description: Exclusion-set membership is by NAME, not by typed knowledge. A consumer recomputing content_hash MUST strip the v0.1.0 exclusion set ({ctx_id, lineage_id, origin_registry, created_at, content_hash, signature}) by exact key name, regardless of whether the consumer's typed model has fields for those names. This fixture demonstrates a stored Body (full retrieval response) whose exclusion-set fields are stripped by name to produce ProducerContent; the resulting hash MUST equal body.content_hash. Pairs with can-008 (which pins the producer-side: unknown producer-controlled fields are INCLUDED).

vectors.name: Full Body → ProducerContent → hash by stripping exclusion set by name
verification_steps: 1. Parse `stored_body` from the wire bytes into a structure that preserves all keys (e.g. serde_json::Value, dict[str, Any]).

2. Build ProducerContent by removing every key whose name appears in the v0.1.0 exclusion set: {ctx_id, lineage_id, origin_registry, created_at, content_hash, signature}. Removal MUST be by string name; the typed kind/content of the value is irrelevant.
vectors.consumer_behavior: REQUIRED. The consumer's exclusion-set logic MUST be a static name list, not a function of which keys the typed model knows about. This is what guarantees a v0.1.0 consumer behaves correctly when the wire body contains the same six exclusion-set names — even if a future minor version adds documentation about new semantics for one of them.
vectors.non_conformant_behavior: A consumer that derives the exclusion set from struct field metadata (e.g., 'fields with #[serde(skip_serializing)]') rather than the spec-defined name list could miscompute when the typed model is patched without updating the exclusion-list logic. The exclusion set is normative, fixed by RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.7, and MUST be implemented as such.

notes: The expected canonical_form and sha256_hex above are also exactly what a producer would have computed before signing (when their request body had no exclusion-set fields yet). This is the symmetric property: producer-side compute and consumer-side recompute MUST yield byte-identical canonical strings.
